user_message: How should I coach David Chen on Litware Inc? We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 10:47 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc consumption trend

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 17:24 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 12:55 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc consumption trend

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-03-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:12:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:16:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:21:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:29:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:34:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:37:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-G failure mode (Misattributes attendee role)

Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
